nameMeanings.name: Pigg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English pigge ‘pig’ (Old English picg(a)), hence a metonymic occupational name for a swineherd or nickname for someone supposedly resembling a hog (see Hogg ).

variants.name: Hogg
meanings: English (northern England and Scotland): nickname for a swineherd or shepherd, from Middle English hog(ge) (Old English hogg) denoting either a pig, especially a castrated one, or a young sheep before its first shearing (the latter sense is most likely in northern England).
German (Högg): topographic name, a variant of Heck 1, found chiefly in Bavaria.

variants.name: Pigue
meanings: French (southern): probably a variant of   Pique   ‘pick’. This surname is very rare in France.

nameMeanings.name: Pidge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a ridge, Middle English rigge (Old English hrycg) ‘ridge’, or a habitational name from any of the minor places so named. It is synonymous with West Midlands Rudge and northern Rigg . The surname is also fairly common in Ireland, in County Galway, having been taken to Connacht in the early 17th century. The name is sometimes Gaelicized as Mac Iomaire; iomaire being modern Irish for ‘ridge’. Compare Attridge .
sometimes perhaps a variant of   Rich   with voicing of the formerly medial consonant.

variants.name: Rigg
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of the many minor placenames in northern England named with Middle English rigg (Old Norse hryggr) ‘ridge’. Compare the synonymous Ridge .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names based on the element rīc ‘power(ful)’. This surname is very rare in Germany.
Altered form of Swiss German Rüegg (see Ruegg ).

variants.name: Ridges
meanings: English (Hampshire and Surrey): variant of Ridge , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

en.lifeSummary: Nicholas Pigg Pidge was born in 1552, in Essex, England, United Kingdom as the son of William Pigg and Edith Cokayne. He married Johanna Adame in 1574, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He died in May 1603, in Newport, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Nicholas Pigg Pidge est né en 1552, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de William Pigg et Edith Cokayne. Il a épousé Johanna Adame en 1574, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en mai 1603, à Newport, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Nicholas Pigg Pidge wurde 1552 in Essex, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von William Pigg und Edith Cokayne geboren. Er hat 1574 in Essex, England Johanna Adame geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb im Mai 1603 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Newport, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Pigg Pidge(은)는 1552년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 William Pigg(와)과 Edith Cokayne의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1574년 영국 에세스에서 Johanna Adame(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1603년 5월 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 뉴포트에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.
